traits.quirk: has an enemy (Birel, a elf outlaw, level 3, out there somewhere)

q01.name: The Grudge War
q01.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q01.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q02.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q02.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q02.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Eberk, age 8)

q03.name: The Grudge War
q03.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q03.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q04.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q04.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q04.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Borin, age 8)

q05.name: The Grudge War
q05.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q05.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q06.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q06.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q06.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q07.name: The Boiler-Cult
q07.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q07.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q08.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q08.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q08.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q09.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q09.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q09.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q10.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q10.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q10.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Nessa, a human rival adventurer, level 10, out there somewhere)

q11.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q11.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q11.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q12.name: The Restless Crypt
q12.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q12.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q13.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q13.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q13.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q14.name: The Proving Hunt
q14.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.
q14.epilogue: The hide hangs in the long-tent and the party's name is spoken at the fire -- iron and standing, paid in full.

q15.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q15.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q15.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q16.name: Rival Warband
q16.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q16.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q17.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q17.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q17.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q18.name: Things in the Deep Roads
q18.desc: The under-way to the second hold is silked and skittering. The toll-guild pays by the cleared mile.
q18.epilogue: The under-way reopens by the cleared mile, tolls half-price for a season in the party's honor.

q19.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q19.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q19.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q20.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q20.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q20.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q21.name: The Restless Crypt
q21.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q21.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q22.name: The Flooded Hold
q22.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q22.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

q23.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q23.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q23.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q24.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q24.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q24.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q25.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q25.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q25.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q26.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q26.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.
q26.epilogue: The seams ring with picks again by week's end.

q27.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q27.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q27.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q28.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q28.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q28.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q29.name: Rival Warband
q29.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q29.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q30.name: Press-Gang Trouble

q30.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q31.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q32.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q32.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q33.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q33.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q33.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q34.name: Ashes Going Home
q34.desc: One of theirs died far from home. The family wants the ashes carried the whole way, and carried right.
q34.epilogue: The urn goes into the ground beside its people; the kin set a place at the table for the party that night.

q35.name: The Sealed Dispatch
q35.desc: Court business under wax, and a rider who never arrived. Someone would rather it never does.
q35.epilogue: The seal is broken behind a shut door; whatever it says, couriers ride the same road twice as guarded within the week.
